message:
fix: shortest_simple_paths missing-source/target wording (br-r37-c1-hpeix)

Probe-driven follow-up to br-r37-c1-jxvsu's missing-node wording
sweep.  ``shortest_simple_paths`` was emitting the title-cased
'Source 99 is not in G' / 'Target 99 is not in G' variant from
the Rust binding, while nx uses lowercase 'source node 99 not
in graph' / 'target node 99 not in graph' (from its underlying
``_bidirectional_shortest_path`` helper).

Drop-in code matching on the message string (log filters, test
assertions) was breaking.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py`` (``shortest_simple_paths``):

- Pre-validate ``source not in G`` and ``target not in G`` BEFORE
  the multigraph guard's delegation / Rust call, raising
  ``NodeNotFound`` with nx's exact wording.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_shortest_simple_paths_msg_parity.py``:
- 2 × missing-source / missing-target wording assertions
- 3 × regression guards (hashable input, callable-weight
  delegation, multigraph @not_implemented_for)

Full sweep
----------
7117 passed, 0 failed (was 7112 before — +5 new regression cases).

Found via systematic error-message probe per the idle-nudge
self-review discipline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -85,6 +85,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-h83lo","title":"parity: bridges yields edges in alphabetical order, nx yields in DFS-discovery order","description":"fnx.bridges returns bridge edges in canonical/alphabetical order ([('a','b'), ('b','c'), ('c','d'), ('d','e')]). nx.bridges returns them in DFS-discovery order ([('c','d'), ('c','b'), ('d','e'), ('a','b')]) which depends on the algorithm's traversal. Drop-in code that iterates the bridges in the algorithm's order — e.g. for visualization or sequential processing — gets different output. Fix: delegate to nx for order parity (Rust impl is per-edge bridge detection, doesn't gain much; the fnx_to_nx converter now correctly preserves adj order so nx's DFS produces consistent results).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:47:02.439979899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:49:20.670533372Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:49:20.668386677Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 6d135a9. bridges delegates to nx for DFS-discovery order parity. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-hiplx","title":"Refactor draw_networkx_* re-exports to be classified as PY_WRAPPER (last 4 NX_DELEGATED)","description":"The 4 remaining NX_DELEGATED items (draw_networkx, draw_networkx_edge_labels, draw_networkx_edges, draw_networkx_nodes) are created via _wrap_nx_drawing using functools.wraps which sets __wrapped__ to the nx function. inspect.getsource follows __wrapped__ and returns nx's source, so the classifier sees nx references and flags as NX_DELEGATED.

Fix: replace functools.wraps with manual attribute copy that doesn't set __wrapped__. Preserve signature introspection (br-r37-c1-hz9k5) by setting __signature__ directly on the wrapper. After this, inspect.getsource returns the wrapper's own source (which doesn't reference nx) and the functions classify as PY_WRAPPER.

Drops NX_DELEGATED count by 4+ (4 → 0). Should also resolve test_public_coverage_has_no_networkx_delegated_exports.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:48:50.671684199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:51:24.590082242Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:51:24.588080153Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-hkbd8","title":"stochastic_block_model nodelist order not preserved when delegating to nx","description":"test_sbm_generators.py::test_stochastic_block_model_fallback_preserves_nodelist_and_attrs is a pre-existing failure asserting that fnx preserves the user-supplied nodelist order:

  fnx.stochastic_block_model([2,1], [[0,0],[0,0]], nodelist=['left','right','solo']) 
  -> expects nodes() == ['left','right','solo']

But fnx delegates to nx via br-sbmrng, and nx's stochastic_block_model uses set-iteration on the partition blocks → returns ['right','left','solo'] (hash order within block 0).

The pre-br-sbmrng fnx native fast path had nodelist-order-preserving semantics, which the test was written to verify.

Fix: after delegating to nx, post-process the result to restore the original nodelist order. Build a new fnx graph that walks the explicit nodelist (with attrs preserved), copying over edges and graph attrs from the nx result.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:03:37.890244324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:10:18.599250009Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:10:18.597255632Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-hpeix","title":"shortest_simple_paths missing-source/target message wording drift","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:53:02.813856531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:53:53.696430008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-hq5pq","title":"parity: connectivity minimum_st_edge_cut missing","description":"networkx.algorithms.connectivity.minimum_st_edge_cut(G, s, t, flow_func=None, auxiliary=None, residual=None) returns minimum st edge cut. Not exposed by franken_networkx (only minimum_edge_cut and minimum_st_node_cut exist). Add Python wrapper delegating to networkx and export at top-level.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:59:43.139262003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:15:05.326488163Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:15:05.324637730Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in fnx — top-level export matches nx.connectivity exactly. Landed tests/python/test_connectivity_parity_extras.py (commit 3765516) with 37 cases freezing the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-hz9k5","title":"parity: drawing wrappers (draw, draw_networkx, draw_networkx_*, display) collapse nx's per-arg signature into generic *args, **kwargs","description":"fnx.draw_networkx, fnx.draw_networkx_nodes, fnx.draw_networkx_edges, fnx.draw_networkx_labels, fnx.draw_networkx_edge_labels, fnx.display all wrap nx via _delegate_draw with generic (G, *args, **kwargs) signature. inspect.signature returns the catch-all instead of nx's documented surface (e.g. nx.draw_networkx_edges has 18 keyword params). Same root cause as approximation namespace fix (functools.wraps). draw also has the variadic-kwarg-name drift (kwargs vs nx's kwds). Fix: apply functools.wraps(nx_func) to each wrapper. Add parity test that asserts inspect.signature(fnx.X) matches inspect.signature(nx.X) for the full drawing namespace.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:08:06.159041751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:10:36.083919851Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:10:36.082061192Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit c36f08f. Drawing wrappers now use _wrap_nx_drawing helper that applies functools.wraps(nx_func) to each delegator. inspect.signature(fnx.draw_networkx_edges) returns the full 18-parameter nx surface. 18 parity tests in test_drawing_signature_parity.py freeze the contract (signature match + namespace check + metadata).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-i8qr5","title":"is_chordal/complete_to_chordal_graph silently accept K4+self-loop where nx raises","description":"fnx.is_chordal and fnx.complete_to_chordal_graph silently succeed on graphs where nx's internal _is_complete_graph helper raises NetworkXError('Self loop found in _is_complete_graph()').

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(0,1),(0,2),(0,3),(1,2),(1,3),(2,3),(0,0)])  # K4 + self-loop
  >>> fnx.is_chordal(g)                       # True
  >>> nx.is_chordal(nx.Graph(g))
  NetworkXError: Self loop found in _is_complete_graph()

  >>> fnx.complete_to_chordal_graph(g)
  (Graph(nodes=4, edges=7), {0: 0, 1: 0, 2: 0, 3: 0})
  >>> nx.complete_to_chordal_graph(nx.Graph(g))
  NetworkXError: Self loop found in _is_complete_graph()

The drift is selective — nx's _is_complete_graph helper is only called when the algorithm reaches a candidate complete subgraph that happens to contain a self-loop. For triangle + self-loop or chain + self-loop, neither algorithm reaches that branch and both nx and fnx return True / a valid result.

Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXError) on the K4+self-loop case won't trigger on fnx. fnx's behavior is more permissive but breaks exact-error parity with nx.

Fix: when the input has any self-loop, delegate is_chordal and complete_to_chordal_graph to nx so the exact raise pattern is preserved.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:57:04.473475048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:04:29.309215871Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:04:29.305534536Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -9370,6 +9370,14 @@ def shortest_simple_paths(G, source, target, weight=None):
     """
     if G.is_multigraph():
         raise NetworkXNotImplemented("not implemented for multigraph type")
+    # br-r37-c1-hpeix: pre-validate so we get nx's exact lowercase
+    # 'source/target node X not in graph' wording.  The Rust
+    # binding was emitting the title-cased 'Source X is not in G'
+    # variant.
+    if source not in G:
+        raise NodeNotFound(f"source node {source} not in graph")
+    if target not in G:
+        raise NodeNotFound(f"target node {target} not in graph")
     if callable(weight):
         yield from _call_networkx_for_parity(
             "shortest_simple_paths", G, source, target, weight=weight

@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+"""Parity for shortest_simple_paths missing-source/target wording.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-hpeix.
+
+Pre-fix the Rust binding emitted::
+
+    NodeNotFound: Source 99 is not in G
+    NodeNotFound: Target 99 is not in G
+
+…while nx uses lowercase title-less wording matching its
+underlying ``_bidirectional_shortest_path`` helper::
+
+    NodeNotFound: source node 99 not in graph
+    NodeNotFound: target node 99 not in graph
+
+Drop-in code that matches on the message string (logging filters,
+test assertions) breaks otherwise.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_shortest_simple_paths_missing_source_message():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NodeNotFound, match=r"^source node 99 not in graph$"):
+        list(fnx.shortest_simple_paths(G, 99, 2))
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NodeNotFound, match=r"^source node 99 not in graph$"):
+        list(nx.shortest_simple_paths(GX, 99, 2))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_shortest_simple_paths_missing_target_message():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NodeNotFound, match=r"^target node 99 not in graph$"):
+        list(fnx.shortest_simple_paths(G, 1, 99))
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NodeNotFound, match=r"^target node 99 not in graph$"):
+        list(nx.shortest_simple_paths(GX, 1, 99))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_shortest_simple_paths_hashable_unchanged():
+    """Real run still yields paths in length order."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (1, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (1, 3)])
+    f = sorted(map(tuple, fnx.shortest_simple_paths(G, 1, 3)), key=len)
+    n = sorted(map(tuple, nx.shortest_simple_paths(GX, 1, 3)), key=len)
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_shortest_simple_paths_callable_weight_unchanged():
+    """Callable weight delegates to nx — verify it still works."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = list(fnx.shortest_simple_paths(G, 1, 3, weight=lambda u, v, d: 1.0))
+    n = list(nx.shortest_simple_paths(GX, 1, 3, weight=lambda u, v, d: 1.0))
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_shortest_simple_paths_multigraph_still_not_implemented():
+    """Multigraph guard stays in place (br-r37-c1-682kr)."""
+    MG = fnx.MultiGraph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXNotImplemented):
+        list(fnx.shortest_simple_paths(MG, 1, 3))
